query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PTFE that can be used in industrial seals applications

properties: Low friction coefficient (≤0.04 at room temperature)
High chemical resistance to acids, bases, solvents, and oils
Thermal stability up to 200 °C continuous service
Good dimensional stability and low compression set (<10 %) over operating life
Adequate mechanical strength for sealing under pressure (tensile strength ≥30 MPa)
Compatibility with typical seal manufacturing processes (extrusion, injection molding, or compression molding)
Non-reactive to common seal lubricants and greases
constraints: Must not degrade or swell in the presence of aggressive chemicals used in the target industrial process
Should maintain sealing integrity at both low (≤0 °C) and high temperatures (up to 200 °C)
Material hardness should be within 70–90 Shore A for typical elastomeric seals
No hazardous by‑products or toxic degradation products during service
Must be available in grades that can be processed at lab scale without specialized equipment

final_candidate.justification: Viton A offers a balance of PTFE‑like chemical resistance and superior mechanical properties. It has a compression set <10 % at 200 °C, tensile strength ~35 MPa, Shore A hardness 70–80, and can be processed by extrusion or injection molding. Its low friction coefficient (~0.04) matches PTFE while providing better sealing performance under pressure. The material is commercially available in lab‑scale grades (e.g., 1‑inch extrudable strands) and has no known toxic degradation products.

process_recipe.description: Blend base FKM polymer with 5–10 wt% of a low‑friction additive (e.g., polytetrafluoroethylene powder) and 2–4 wt% of a crosslinking agent (peroxide).
process_recipe.conditions: Mix in a twin‑screw extruder at 260–280 °C, 50–70 rpm.

process_recipe.description: Extrude the compounded material into strands or sheets suitable for compression molding.
Compression mold the strands into seal geometry at 260 °C for 30 s, then cool under pressure to room temperature.
process_recipe.conditions: Mold pressure 5–10 MPa, cooling time 2 min.

process_recipe.description: Post‑cure the molded seal in an oven at 150 °C for 1 h to complete peroxide crosslinking.
